category|Category:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:三宅神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11355073}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miyake Shrine |画像 = [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|30.74|N|135|35|21.17|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miyake Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Wakumusubi||WD=|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miyake Inari / Mure Inari / Kusawake Inari |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686}}[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380103-01.html Miyake Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki shrines, personal site). |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Miyake Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine''' (みやけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Miyake-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called "Miyake Inari," and was formerly called "Mure Inari" and "Kusawake Inari." === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}''' (うかのみたまのみこと)Deities from precinct information board. * '''{{ill|lt=Wakumusubi||WD=|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}''' (わくむすびのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}}''' (とようけもちのみこと) All three are deities related to grain. === History === [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Precinct
Honden in center, maiden on left, Toyouke Ōkami on right.]] Founding is unknown. It is said to derive from establishment of a {{ill|lt=miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761}} (imperial direct estate) in this area. Though local relation is unclear, the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' records that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}} 2 ({{ill|lt=535|Q31019|WD=Q31019}}?) a miyake called "Soshiki" was established in Tanba'Nihon Shoki,' Emperor Ankan year 2, fifth month entry: "... established Soshiki miyake in Tanba Province." ([https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_18.html Nihon Shoki Vol.18 Emperor Ankan Chronicle] via full-text search).. It is also argued that this area was the center where the Kuwata district office stood, based on old reading of district office ({{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}) as "kōri no miyake," the broad flat terrain, and presence of this shrine suggesting miyake existenceFrom precinct information board.'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' entries for Miyake village and Miyake Shrine.. Many kiln remains, thought to have produced pottery for rituals, have been found nearby[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/kameoka/06.html#k059 Miyake Shrine] (Tanba shrines, personal site).. In the mid-Heian '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Miyake Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}, and ranked as a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. It is said to have once held a vast shrine precinct of one chō square, but shrank due to war fires in the Tenshō era and other fires including {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577}}). The current shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1673|Q7617|WD=Q7617}}), and an outer covering structure was added in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|WD=Q7706}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 40 became a designated village shrine. === Sessha/Massha === * Toyouke Ōkami * Inari-sha === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō '''Access''' * Nearest stations: {{ill|lt=Kameoka Station|Q1043696|WD=Q1043696}} or {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} on {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} ({{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}), about 15 minutes on foot '''Nearby''' * Site of {{ill|lt=Kameyama Castle (Tanba)|Q1037429|WD=Q1037429|lt=Kameyama Castle}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Precinct information board * 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Miyake Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:三宅神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11355073}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miyake Shrine |画像 = [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|30.74|N|135|35|21.17|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miyake Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Wakumusubi||WD=|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miyake Inari / Mure Inari / Kusawake Inari |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686}}[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380103-01.html Miyake Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki shrines, personal site). |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Miyake Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine''' (みやけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Miyake-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called "Miyake Inari," and was formerly called "Mure Inari" and "Kusawake Inari." === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}''' (うかのみたまのみこと)Deities from precinct information board. * '''{{ill|lt=Wakumusubi||WD=|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}''' (わくむすびのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}}''' (とようけもちのみこと) All three are deities related to grain. === History === [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Precinct
Honden in center, maiden on left, Toyouke Ōkami on right.]] Founding is unknown. It is said to derive from establishment of a {{ill|lt=miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761}} (imperial direct estate) in this area. Though local relation is unclear, the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' records that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}} 2 ({{ill|lt=535|Q31019|WD=Q31019}}?) a miyake called "Soshiki" was established in Tanba'Nihon Shoki,' Emperor Ankan year 2, fifth month entry: "... established Soshiki miyake in Tanba Province." ([https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_18.html Nihon Shoki Vol.18 Emperor Ankan Chronicle] via full-text search).. It is also argued that this area was the center where the Kuwata district office stood, based on old reading of district office ({{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}) as "kōri no miyake," the broad flat terrain, and presence of this shrine suggesting miyake existenceFrom precinct information board.'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' entries for Miyake village and Miyake Shrine.. Many kiln remains, thought to have produced pottery for rituals, have been found nearby[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/kameoka/06.html#k059 Miyake Shrine] (Tanba shrines, personal site).. In the mid-Heian '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Miyake Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. It is said to have once held a vast shrine precinct of one chō square, but shrank due to war fires in the Tenshō era and other fires including {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577}}). The current shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1673|Q7617|WD=Q7617}}), and an outer covering structure was added in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|WD=Q7706}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 40 became a designated village shrine. === Sessha/Massha === * Toyouke Ōkami * Inari-sha === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō '''Access''' * Nearest stations: {{ill|lt=Kameoka Station|Q1043696|WD=Q1043696}} or {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} on {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} ({{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}), about 15 minutes on foot '''Nearby''' * Site of {{ill|lt=Kameyama Castle (Tanba)|Q1037429|WD=Q1037429|lt=Kameyama Castle}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Precinct information board * 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Miyake Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:三宅神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11355073}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miyake Shrine |画像 = [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|30.74|N|135|35|21.17|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miyake Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Wakumusubi||WD=|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miyake Inari / Mure Inari / Kusawake Inari |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686}}[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380103-01.html Miyake Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki shrines, personal site). |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Miyake Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine''' (みやけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Miyake-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called "Miyake Inari," and was formerly called "Mure Inari" and "Kusawake Inari." === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}''' (うかのみたまのみこと)Deities from precinct information board. * '''{{ill|lt=Wakumusubi||WD=|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}''' (わくむすびのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}}''' (とようけもちのみこと) All three are deities related to grain. === History === [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Precinct
Honden in center, maiden on left, Toyouke Ōkami on right.]] Founding is unknown. It is said to derive from establishment of a {{ill|lt=miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761}} (imperial direct estate) in this area. Though local relation is unclear, the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' records that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}} 2 ({{ill|lt=535|Q31019|WD=Q31019}}?) a miyake called "Soshiki" was established in Tanba'Nihon Shoki,' Emperor Ankan year 2, fifth month entry: "... established Soshiki miyake in Tanba Province." ([https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_18.html Nihon Shoki Vol.18 Emperor Ankan Chronicle] via full-text search).. It is also argued that this area was the center where the Kuwata district office stood, based on old reading of district office ({{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}) as "kōri no miyake," the broad flat terrain, and presence of this shrine suggesting miyake existenceFrom precinct information board.'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' entries for Miyake village and Miyake Shrine.. Many kiln remains, thought to have produced pottery for rituals, have been found nearby[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/kameoka/06.html#k059 Miyake Shrine] (Tanba shrines, personal site).. In the mid-Heian '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Miyake Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is said to have once held a vast shrine precinct of one chō square, but shrank due to war fires in the Tenshō era and other fires including {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577}}). The current shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1673|Q7617|WD=Q7617}}), and an outer covering structure was added in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|WD=Q7706}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 40 became a designated village shrine. === Sessha/Massha === * Toyouke Ōkami * Inari-sha === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō '''Access''' * Nearest stations: {{ill|lt=Kameoka Station|Q1043696|WD=Q1043696}} or {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} on {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} ({{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}), about 15 minutes on foot '''Nearby''' * Site of {{ill|lt=Kameyama Castle (Tanba)|Q1037429|WD=Q1037429|lt=Kameyama Castle}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Precinct information board * 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Miyake Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:三宅神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11355073}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miyake Shrine |画像 = [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|30.74|N|135|35|21.17|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miyake Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miyake Inari / Mure Inari / Kusawake Inari |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686}}[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380103-01.html Miyake Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki shrines, personal site). |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Miyake Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine''' (みやけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Miyake-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called "Miyake Inari," and was formerly called "Mure Inari" and "Kusawake Inari." === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}''' (うかのみたまのみこと)Deities from precinct information board. * '''{{ill|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}''' (わくむすびのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}}''' (とようけもちのみこと) All three are deities related to grain. === History === [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Precinct
Honden in center, maiden on left, Toyouke Ōkami on right.]] Founding is unknown. It is said to derive from establishment of a {{ill|lt=miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761}} (imperial direct estate) in this area. Though local relation is unclear, the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' records that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}} 2 ({{ill|lt=535|Q31019|WD=Q31019}}?) a miyake called "Soshiki" was established in Tanba'Nihon Shoki,' Emperor Ankan year 2, fifth month entry: "... established Soshiki miyake in Tanba Province." ([https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_18.html Nihon Shoki Vol.18 Emperor Ankan Chronicle] via full-text search).. It is also argued that this area was the center where the Kuwata district office stood, based on old reading of district office ({{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}) as "kōri no miyake," the broad flat terrain, and presence of this shrine suggesting miyake existenceFrom precinct information board.'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' entries for Miyake village and Miyake Shrine.. Many kiln remains, thought to have produced pottery for rituals, have been found nearby[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/kameoka/06.html#k059 Miyake Shrine] (Tanba shrines, personal site).. In the mid-Heian '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Miyake Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is said to have once held a vast shrine precinct of one chō square, but shrank due to war fires in the Tenshō era and other fires including {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577}}). The current shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1673|Q7617|WD=Q7617}}), and an outer covering structure was added in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|WD=Q7706}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 40 became a designated village shrine. === Sessha/Massha === * Toyouke Ōkami * Inari-sha === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō '''Access''' * Nearest stations: {{ill|lt=Kameoka Station|Q1043696|WD=Q1043696}} or {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} on {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} ({{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}), about 15 minutes on foot '''Nearby''' * Site of {{ill|lt=Kameyama Castle (Tanba)|Q1037429|WD=Q1037429|lt=Kameyama Castle}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Precinct information board * 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Miyake Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:三宅神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11355073}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miyake Shrine |画像 = [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|30.74|N|135|35|21.17|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miyake Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miyake Inari / Mure Inari / Kusawake Inari |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686}}[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380103-01.html Miyake Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki shrines, personal site). |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Miyake Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine''' (みやけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Miyake-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called "Miyake Inari," and was formerly called "Mure Inari" and "Kusawake Inari." === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}''' (うかのみたまのみこと)Deities from precinct information board. * '''{{ill|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}''' (わくむすびのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}}''' (とようけもちのみこと) All three are deities related to grain. === History === [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Precinct
Honden in center, maiden on left, Toyouke Ōkami on right.]] Founding is unknown. It is said to derive from establishment of a {{ill|lt=miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761}} (imperial direct estate) in this area. Though local relation is unclear, the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' records that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}} 2 ({{ill|lt=535|Q31019|WD=Q31019}}?) a miyake called "Soshiki" was established in Tanba'Nihon Shoki,' Emperor Ankan year 2, fifth month entry: "... established Soshiki miyake in Tanba Province." ([https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_18.html Nihon Shoki Vol.18 Emperor Ankan Chronicle] via full-text search).. It is also argued that this area was the center where the Kuwata district office stood, based on old reading of district office ({{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}) as "kōri no miyake," the broad flat terrain, and presence of this shrine suggesting miyake existenceFrom precinct information board.'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' entries for Miyake village and Miyake Shrine.. Many kiln remains, thought to have produced pottery for rituals, have been found nearby[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/kameoka/06.html#k059 Miyake Shrine] (Tanba shrines, personal site).. In the mid-Heian '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Miyake Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is said to have once held a vast shrine precinct of one chō square, but shrank due to war fires in the Tenshō era and other fires including {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577}}). The current shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1673|Q7617|WD=Q7617}}), and an outer covering structure was added in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|WD=Q7706}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 40 became a designated village shrine. === Sessha/Massha === * Toyouke Ōkami * Inari-sha === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō '''Access''' * Nearest stations: {{ill|lt=Kameoka Station|Q1043696|WD=Q1043696}} or {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} on {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} ({{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}), about 15 minutes on foot '''Nearby''' * Site of {{ill|lt=Kameyama Castle (Tanba)|Q1037429|WD=Q1037429|lt=Kameyama Castle}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Precinct information board * 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Miyake Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:三宅神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11355073}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miyake Shrine |画像 = [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|30.74|N|135|35|21.17|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miyake Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miyake Inari / Mure Inari / Kusawake Inari |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686}}[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380103-01.html Miyake Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki shrines, personal site). |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Miyake Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine''' (みやけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Miyake-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called "Miyake Inari," and was formerly called "Mure Inari" and "Kusawake Inari." === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}''' (うかのみたまのみこと)Deities from precinct information board. * '''{{ill|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}''' (わくむすびのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}}''' (とようけもちのみこと) All three are deities related to grain. === History === [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Precinct
Honden in center, maiden on left, Toyouke Ōkami on right.]] Founding is unknown. It is said to derive from establishment of a {{ill|lt=miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761}} (imperial direct estate) in this area. Though local relation is unclear, the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' records that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}} 2 ({{ill|lt=535|Q31019|WD=Q31019}}?) a miyake called "Soshiki" was established in Tanba'Nihon Shoki,' Emperor Ankan year 2, fifth month entry: "... established Soshiki miyake in Tanba Province." ([https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_18.html Nihon Shoki Vol.18 Emperor Ankan Chronicle] via full-text search).. It is also argued that this area was the center where the Kuwata district office stood, based on old reading of district office ({{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}) as "kōri no miyake," the broad flat terrain, and presence of this shrine suggesting miyake existenceFrom precinct information board.'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' entries for Miyake village and Miyake Shrine.. Many kiln remains, thought to have produced pottery for rituals, have been found nearby[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/kameoka/06.html#k059 Miyake Shrine] (Tanba shrines, personal site).. In the mid-Heian '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Miyake Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have once held a vast shrine precinct of one chō square, but shrank due to war fires in the Tenshō era and other fires including {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577}}). The current shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1673|Q7617|WD=Q7617}}), and an outer covering structure was added in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|WD=Q7706}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 40 became a designated village shrine. === Sessha/Massha === * Toyouke Ōkami * Inari-sha === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō '''Access''' * Nearest stations: {{ill|lt=Kameoka Station|Q1043696|WD=Q1043696}} or {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} on {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} ({{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}), about 15 minutes on foot '''Nearby''' * Site of {{ill|lt=Kameyama Castle (Tanba)|Q1037429|WD=Q1037429|lt=Kameyama Castle}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Precinct information board * 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Miyake Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:三宅神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11355073}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miyake Shrine |画像 = [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|30.74|N|135|35|21.17|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miyake Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miyake Inari / Mure Inari / Kusawake Inari |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686}}[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380103-01.html Miyake Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki shrines, personal site). |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Miyake Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine''' (みやけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Miyake-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called "Miyake Inari," and was formerly called "Mure Inari" and "Kusawake Inari." === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}''' (うかのみたまのみこと)Deities from precinct information board. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}''' (わくむすびのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}}''' (とようけもちのみこと) All three are deities related to grain. === History === [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Precinct
Honden in center, maiden on left, Toyouke Ōkami on right.]] Founding is unknown. It is said to derive from establishment of a {{ill|lt=miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761}} (imperial direct estate) in this area. Though local relation is unclear, the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' records that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}} 2 ({{ill|lt=535|Q31019|WD=Q31019}}?) a miyake called "Soshiki" was established in Tanba'Nihon Shoki,' Emperor Ankan year 2, fifth month entry: "... established Soshiki miyake in Tanba Province." ([https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_18.html Nihon Shoki Vol.18 Emperor Ankan Chronicle] via full-text search).. It is also argued that this area was the center where the Kuwata district office stood, based on old reading of district office ({{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}) as "kōri no miyake," the broad flat terrain, and presence of this shrine suggesting miyake existenceFrom precinct information board.'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' entries for Miyake village and Miyake Shrine.. Many kiln remains, thought to have produced pottery for rituals, have been found nearby[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/kameoka/06.html#k059 Miyake Shrine] (Tanba shrines, personal site).. In the mid-Heian '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Miyake Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have once held a vast shrine precinct of one chō square, but shrank due to war fires in the Tenshō era and other fires including {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577}}). The current shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1673|Q7617|WD=Q7617}}), and an outer covering structure was added in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|WD=Q7706}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 40 became a designated village shrine. === Sessha/Massha === * Toyouke Ōkami * Inari-sha === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō '''Access''' * Nearest stations: {{ill|lt=Kameoka Station|Q1043696|WD=Q1043696}} or {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} on {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} ({{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}), about 15 minutes on foot '''Nearby''' * Site of {{ill|lt=Kameyama Castle (Tanba)|Q1037429|WD=Q1037429|lt=Kameyama Castle}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Precinct information board * 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Miyake Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:三宅神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11355073}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miyake Shrine |画像 = [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|30.74|N|135|35|21.17|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miyake Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miyake Inari / Mure Inari / Kusawake Inari |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686}}[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380103-01.html Miyake Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki shrines, personal site). |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Miyake Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine''' (みやけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Miyake-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called "Miyake Inari," and was formerly called "Mure Inari" and "Kusawake Inari." === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}''' (うかのみたまのみこと)Deities from precinct information board. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}''' (わくむすびのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}}''' (とようけもちのみこと) All three are deities related to grain. === History === [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Precinct
Honden in center, maiden on left, Toyouke Ōkami on right.]] Founding is unknown. It is said to derive from establishment of a {{ill|lt=miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761}} (imperial direct estate) in this area. Though local relation is unclear, the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' records that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}} 2 ({{ill|lt=535|Q31019|WD=Q31019}}?) a miyake called "Soshiki" was established in Tanba'Nihon Shoki,' Emperor Ankan year 2, fifth month entry: "... established Soshiki miyake in Tanba Province." ([https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_18.html Nihon Shoki Vol.18 Emperor Ankan Chronicle] via full-text search).. It is also argued that this area was the center where the Kuwata district office stood, based on old reading of district office ({{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}) as "kōri no miyake," the broad flat terrain, and presence of this shrine suggesting miyake existenceFrom precinct information board.'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' entries for Miyake village and Miyake Shrine.. Many kiln remains, thought to have produced pottery for rituals, have been found nearby[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/kameoka/06.html#k059 Miyake Shrine] (Tanba shrines, personal site).. In the mid-Heian '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Miyake Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have once held a vast shrine precinct of one chō square, but shrank due to war fires in the Tenshō era and other fires including {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577}}). The current shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1673|Q7617|WD=Q7617}}), and an outer covering structure was added in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|WD=Q7706}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 40 became a designated village shrine. === Sessha/Massha === * Toyouke Ōkami * Inari-sha === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō '''Access''' * Nearest stations: {{ill|lt=Kameoka Station|Q1043696|WD=Q1043696}} or {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} on {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} ({{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}), about 15 minutes on foot '''Nearby''' * Site of {{ill|lt=Kameyama Castle (Tanba)|Q1037429|WD=Q1037429|lt=Kameyama Castle}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Precinct information board * 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Miyake Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:三宅神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11355073}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miyake Shrine |画像 = [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|30.74|N|135|35|21.17|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miyake Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miyake Inari / Mure Inari / Kusawake Inari |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686}}[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380103-01.html Miyake Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki shrines, personal site). |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Miyake Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine''' (みやけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Miyake-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called "Miyake Inari," and was formerly called "Mure Inari" and "Kusawake Inari." === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}''' (うかのみたまのみこと)Deities from precinct information board. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto}}''' (わくむすびのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto}}''' (とようけもちのみこと) All three are deities related to grain. === History === [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Precinct
Honden in center, maiden on left, Toyouke Ōkami on right.]] Founding is unknown. It is said to derive from establishment of a {{ill|lt=miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761}} (imperial direct estate) in this area. Though local relation is unclear, the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' records that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}} 2 ({{ill|lt=535|Q31019|WD=Q31019}}?) a miyake called "Soshiki" was established in Tanba'Nihon Shoki,' Emperor Ankan year 2, fifth month entry: "... established Soshiki miyake in Tanba Province." ([https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_18.html Nihon Shoki Vol.18 Emperor Ankan Chronicle] via full-text search).. It is also argued that this area was the center where the Kuwata district office stood, based on old reading of district office ({{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}) as "kōri no miyake," the broad flat terrain, and presence of this shrine suggesting miyake existenceFrom precinct information board.'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' entries for Miyake village and Miyake Shrine.. Many kiln remains, thought to have produced pottery for rituals, have been found nearby[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/kameoka/06.html#k059 Miyake Shrine] (Tanba shrines, personal site).. In the mid-Heian '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Miyake Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have once held a vast shrine precinct of one chō square, but shrank due to war fires in the Tenshō era and other fires including {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577}}). The current shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1673|Q7617|WD=Q7617}}), and an outer covering structure was added in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|WD=Q7706}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 40 became a designated village shrine. === Sessha/Massha === * Toyouke Ōkami * Inari-sha === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}121 Miyake-chō '''Access''' * Nearest stations: {{ill|lt=Kameoka Station|Q1043696|WD=Q1043696}} or {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} on {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} ({{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}), about 15 minutes on foot '''Nearby''' * Site of {{ill|lt=Kameyama Castle (Tanba)|Q1037429|WD=Q1037429|lt=Kameyama Castle}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Precinct information board * 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Miyake Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2130005008024 == Commons category (P373) == * Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府亀岡市三宅町121', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:三宅神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11355073}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miyake Shrine |画像 = [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}121 Miyake-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|30.74|N|135|35|21.17|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miyake Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto|1=Ukemochi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miyake Inari / Mure Inari / Kusawake Inari |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686|1=July 15}}[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380103-01.html Miyake Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki shrines, personal site). |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Miyake Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine''' (みやけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyake-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also called "Miyake Inari," and was formerly called "Mure Inari" and "Kusawake Inari." === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}''' (うかのみたまのみこと)Deities from precinct information board. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Wakumusubi}}''' (わくむすびのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto|1=Ukemochi}}''' (とようけもちのみこと) All three are deities related to grain. === History === [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Precinct
Honden in center, maiden on left, Toyouke Ōkami on right.]] Founding is unknown. It is said to derive from establishment of a {{ill|lt=miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} (imperial direct estate) in this area. Though local relation is unclear, the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' records that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=535|Q31019|WD=Q31019|1=535}}?) a miyake called "Soshiki" was established in Tanba'Nihon Shoki,' Emperor Ankan year 2, fifth month entry: "... established Soshiki miyake in Tanba Province." ([https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_18.html Nihon Shoki Vol.18 Emperor Ankan Chronicle] via full-text search).. It is also argued that this area was the center where the Kuwata district office stood, based on old reading of district office ({{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230|1=Gunga (Japan)}}) as "kōri no miyake," the broad flat terrain, and presence of this shrine suggesting miyake existenceFrom precinct information board.'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' entries for Miyake village and Miyake Shrine.. Many kiln remains, thought to have produced pottery for rituals, have been found nearby[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/kameoka/06.html#k059 Miyake Shrine] (Tanba shrines, personal site).. In the mid-Heian '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Miyake Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have once held a vast shrine precinct of one chō square, but shrank due to war fires in the Tenshō era and other fires including {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577|1=1663}}). The current shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1673|Q7617|WD=Q7617|1=1673}}), and an outer covering structure was added in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|WD=Q7706|1=1698}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and in Meiji 40 became a designated village shrine. === Sessha/Massha === * Toyouke Ōkami * Inari-sha === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}121 Miyake-chō '''Access''' * Nearest stations: {{ill|lt=Kameoka Station|Q1043696|WD=Q1043696|1=Kameoka Station}} or {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} ({{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}), about 15 minutes on foot '''Nearby''' * Site of {{ill|lt=Kameyama Castle (Tanba)|Q1037429|WD=Q1037429|lt=Kameyama Castle|1=Kameyama Castle (Kyoto)}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Precinct information board * 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Miyake Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2130005008024 == Commons category (P373) == * Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府亀岡市三宅町121', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:三宅神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11355073}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miyake Shrine |画像 = [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}121 Miyake-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|30.74|N|135|35|21.17|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miyake Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto|1=Ukemochi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miyake Inari / Mure Inari / Kusawake Inari |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686|1=July 15}}[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380103-01.html Miyake Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki shrines, personal site). |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Miyake Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine''' (みやけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyake-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also called "Miyake Inari," and was formerly called "Mure Inari" and "Kusawake Inari." === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}''' (うかのみたまのみこと)Deities from precinct information board. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Wakumusubi}}''' (わくむすびのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto|1=Ukemochi}}''' (とようけもちのみこと) All three are deities related to grain. === History === [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Precinct
Honden in center, maiden on left, Toyouke Ōkami on right.]] Founding is unknown. It is said to derive from establishment of a {{ill|lt=miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} (imperial direct estate) in this area. Though local relation is unclear, the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' records that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=535|Q31019|WD=Q31019|1=535}}?) a miyake called "Soshiki" was established in Tanba'Nihon Shoki,' Emperor Ankan year 2, fifth month entry: "... established Soshiki miyake in Tanba Province." ([https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_18.html Nihon Shoki Vol.18 Emperor Ankan Chronicle] via full-text search).. It is also argued that this area was the center where the Kuwata district office stood, based on old reading of district office ({{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230|1=Gunga (Japan)}}) as "kōri no miyake," the broad flat terrain, and presence of this shrine suggesting miyake existenceFrom precinct information board.'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' entries for Miyake village and Miyake Shrine.. Many kiln remains, thought to have produced pottery for rituals, have been found nearby[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/kameoka/06.html#k059 Miyake Shrine] (Tanba shrines, personal site).. In the mid-Heian '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Miyake Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have once held a vast shrine precinct of one chō square, but shrank due to war fires in the Tenshō era and other fires including {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577|1=1663}}). The current shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1673|Q7617|WD=Q7617|1=1673}}), and an outer covering structure was added in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|WD=Q7706|1=1698}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and in Meiji 40 became a designated village shrine. === Sessha/Massha === * Toyouke Ōkami * Inari-sha === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}121 Miyake-chō '''Access''' * Nearest stations: {{ill|lt=Kameoka Station|Q1043696|WD=Q1043696|1=Kameoka Station}} or {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} ({{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}), about 15 minutes on foot '''Nearby''' * Site of {{ill|lt=Kameyama Castle (Tanba)|Q1037429|WD=Q1037429|lt=Kameyama Castle|1=Kameyama Castle (Kyoto)}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Precinct information board * 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Miyake Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2130005008024 == Commons category (P373) == * Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府亀岡市三宅町121', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:三宅神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11355073}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miyake Shrine |画像 = [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}121 Miyake-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|30.74|N|135|35|21.17|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miyake Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto|1=Ukemochi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miyake Inari / Mure Inari / Kusawake Inari |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686|1=July 15}}[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380103-01.html Miyake Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki shrines, personal site). |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Miyake Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine''' (みやけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyake-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also called "Miyake Inari," and was formerly called "Mure Inari" and "Kusawake Inari." === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}''' (うかのみたまのみこと)Deities from precinct information board. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Wakumusubi}}''' (わくむすびのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Toyoukemochi-no-Mikoto|1=Ukemochi}}''' (とようけもちのみこと) All three are deities related to grain. === History === [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Precinct
Honden in center, maiden on left, Toyouke Ōkami on right.]] Founding is unknown. It is said to derive from establishment of a {{ill|lt=miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} (imperial direct estate) in this area. Though local relation is unclear, the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' records that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=535|Q31019|WD=Q31019|1=535}}?) a miyake called "Soshiki" was established in Tanba'Nihon Shoki,' Emperor Ankan year 2, fifth month entry: "... established Soshiki miyake in Tanba Province." ([https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_18.html Nihon Shoki Vol.18 Emperor Ankan Chronicle] via full-text search).. It is also argued that this area was the center where the Kuwata district office stood, based on old reading of district office ({{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230|1=Gunga (Japan)}}) as "kōri no miyake," the broad flat terrain, and presence of this shrine suggesting miyake existenceFrom precinct information board.'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' entries for Miyake village and Miyake Shrine.. Many kiln remains, thought to have produced pottery for rituals, have been found nearby[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/kameoka/06.html#k059 Miyake Shrine] (Tanba shrines, personal site).. In the mid-Heian '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Miyake Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have once held a vast shrine precinct of one chō square, but shrank due to war fires in the Tenshō era and other fires including {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577|1=1663}}). The current shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1673|Q7617|WD=Q7617|1=1673}}), and an outer covering structure was added in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|WD=Q7706|1=1698}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and in Meiji 40 became a designated village shrine. === Sessha/Massha === * Toyouke Ōkami * Inari-sha === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}121 Miyake-chō '''Access''' * Nearest stations: {{ill|lt=Kameoka Station|Q1043696|WD=Q1043696|1=Kameoka Station}} or {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} ({{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}), about 15 minutes on foot '''Nearby''' * Site of {{ill|lt=Kameyama Castle (Tanba)|Q1037429|WD=Q1037429|lt=Kameyama Castle|1=Kameyama Castle (Kyoto)}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Precinct information board * 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Miyake Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miyake Shrine (Q11355073)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
